pi_items.source: Web search and direct HTTP access confirmed the URL is real, publicly accessible, and designed specifically for web scraping practice (demo/sandbox website with ~1000 books across 50 pages)
Valid Linux home directory path for user 'ga' (confirmed in env.json). .seospiderconfig is the correct Screaming Frog configuration file extension.
Valid Linux home directory path for user 'ga'. CSV is a standard export format for Screaming Frog crawl data.
Valid Linux home directory path for user 'ga' with proper directory structure for storing screenshots.
Valid Linux home directory path for user 'ga' with proper directory structure for storing screenshots.

Verified via Screaming Frog documentation (https://www.screamingfrog.co.uk/seo-spider/user-guide/configuration/) that custom user-agent strings are supported. 'AuditBot' is a valid custom user-agent value that matches the task description requirement of 'AuditBot 1.0'.

Verified via Screaming Frog documentation (https://www.screamingfrog.co.uk/seo-spider/user-guide/configuration/) that Max Threads can be configured. Default is 5 threads, but can be set to 1 for conservative 'polite' crawling. This is a valid configuration value.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses a real, publicly accessible website (books.toscrape.com) specifically designed for web scraping practice, containing ~1000 books. Screaming Frog SEO Spider (version 23.2) is a legitimate, real tool that supports all required configuration options: Max Threads adjustable to 1 (default 5), Max URLs/s (referred to as Max URI/s) configuration available, and custom User-Agent strings supported. All file paths conform to standard Linux home directory conventions for user 'ga'. The 'polite' crawling profile uses Max Threads=1 and custom User-Agent 'AuditBot 1.0' for respectful server access.
